task_completion.description: Custom threshold range 1500 to 3071 HU is applied for segmentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius GUI (segmentation/threshold panel), the minimum threshold is set to 1500 and the maximum is set to 3071.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface is generated from the mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model is visible in the bottom-right 3D viewer pane of InVesalius, showing only dense structures like teeth and thick bone.

task_completion.description: The 3D surface is exported as an STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'dense_structures.stl' is visible in the /home/ga/Documents directory in a file manager, terminal, or an InVesalius export dialog.

task_completion.description: The surface volume is recorded in a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file 'dense_volume_report.txt' containing the text 'Volume: X.X mL' (where X.X is a number, expected around 28.7).

task_completion.description: The InVesalius project is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius window title bar displays 'dense_analysis.inv3' or the file is visible in the /home/ga/Documents directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the InVesalius application interface (adjusting thresholds, creating surfaces) rather than just terminal commands creating mock files.

integrity.description: The recorded volume is a genuine measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The volume value in the text file is a specific decimal number (around 28.7 mL), indicating it was read from the software's surface properties rather than fabricated.

integrity.description: The 3D surface matches the requested high-density structures.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows sparse structures (teeth, petrous bone) and NOT a full complete skull, confirming the threshold was actually applied to the 3D surface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target HU range is 1500 to 3071. The expected volume of the 3D surface is approximately 28.7 mL. The resulting 3D surface should appear sparse, primarily showing teeth (enamel) and dense bone (petrous bone), rather than a complete skull.
